dialogos.texto_en: What are you doing, Itsuki?
Uesugi-kun?\nAre you alone?
Yes, why?
...Indeed, it seems like there's no one else here.
Ah, Futaro!
What's up, Miku%p-2;――
There's something I really want to tell you.\nListen.
Whaaaa? W-wait a sec, calm down!\nYou're too close!
Manatees, those manatees%p-2;――
Huh?
They're so cute.\nThey're totally, undeniably cute!
...Manatees?
Yes, manatees.
I-I see.\nI get it, just back off, please.
(*sigh* Miku seems unusually excited.\n Am I misunderstanding something...\n No, I'm just being self-conscious!)
However, are they really cute enough\nto get so excited about?
They're cute.\nTheir movements are just too cute.
When I first saw them,\nI didn't get it either%p-2;――
Yet, swimming with a gentle sway, using their flippers\nand tail to move through the water,\ntheir appearance is indescribably adorable!
Watching them swim, time flies by.
Yes, I could watch them forever!
The manatees you guys are talking about\nare the animals that have\nbecome the basis of mermaid legends, right?
Yes, that's why Miku and I went to see them.\nWe were curious if they really looked like mermaids.
To be honest, they didn't really look like mermaids.\nThey were bigger than I imagined.
But their movements were really cute!\nDespite their large bodies,\nthey flap their fins and swam with all their might!
I-I see...
It was just feeding time,\nand they were fed carrots by their trainer.
They held the carrots in their fins and chomped on them.\nThey even stood on their tails in the water.
Ah, that's right.
Huh? That's it?
I feel like I read something about that before%p-2;――
It seems that the sight of a manatee\nsticking its head out of the water and grabbing food\nwas mistaken for a person.
Indeed, when viewed from above,\nit's possible to mistake them for a person.
From above?\nDidn't you see them in the tank?
The pool where the manatees are kept\ncan be viewed from above on the first floor\nand from the side on the basement level.
Oh, that sounds interesting.
The way manatees are, they make you smile\nno matter what angle you look at them from.
Oh, really?
Futaro, you're not reacting much.\nThey were SO cute, okay?
Well, from how you guys were acting, I could tell\nquite well how cute manatees are.
If you could tell, you should be more reactive.
What am I supposed to do...?
The manatees seemed like they were friendly\nwith the caretaker.\nThey might be easy to tame.
Plus, they might resemble Takeda Shingen a bit.\nDespite having a grandfather-like bearded appearance,\nthey have round, baby-like eyes...
That expression... I can't sympathize well.
If I have time, I'd like to visit them again.
From what you're telling me,\nmanatees seem pretty human-like.
When I stared at it, it came closer and\nstared back at me through the tank.
Perhaps, it noticed my gaze, and that's why it did that?
They don't look like mermaids at all,\nbut with that kind of behavior,\nit's easy to get the wrong idea.
In terms of how they look. In the first place,\nthe typical mermaid with a female upper body\nwas something people imagined in the past.
Even before that, they were depicted\nas creatures with bird bodies and human faces,\nor as giant fish with the face of a cow.
That would...probably not be so cute.
The manatees seemed like they were friendly\nwith the caretaker.\nThey might be easy to tame.
Plus, they might resemble Takeda Shingen a bit.\nDespite having a grandfather-like bearded appearance,\nthey have round, baby-like eyes...
That expression... I can't sympathize well.
If I have time, I'd like to visit them again.
Those manatees, they must have been really cute.\nSeeing you get more and more excited watching them\nwas quite the spectacle.
Huh?\nWas I that excited?
Woah, woah. You were bouncing off the walls.\nI was wondering what was going on at first.
Hehehe, Miku's usually pretty reserved, right?
By the way, you were just as excited as Miku, you know?
R-really?\nWas I being too loud?
Well, don't worry about it.\nAs long as you had fun, that's all that matters.
Yeah, I had fun.
Definitely.
The manatees seemed like they were friendly\nwith the caretaker.\nManatees seem to be pretty easy to tame.
Plus, they might resemble Takeda Shingen a bit.\nDespite having a grandfather-like bearded appearance,\nthey have round, baby-like eyes...
That expression... I can't sympathize well.
If I have time, I'd like to visit them again.
During free time, you can do whatever you want, right?\nI'm going to take a look around here for a bit too.
Futaro, where are you going?\nMaybe I'll go with you.
A-ahh, well, um...
I-I'm planning to do some reconnaissance\nat the aquarium, so I'll be fine on my own.
Hmmm...
Reconnaissance?
I've heard it's a very big aquarium,\nso I wanna at least get a rough idea of what's there.\nSee you later.
Okay. Bye then, Futaro.
